qas.a: 4.1.6. Refers to Attachment 4 Personal Equipment for FARP an ABFDS Specialists, Special Purpose Clothing and Personal Equipment, AFE, Guardian Angel and Special Tactics, AFI 11-301 V1, AFE Program and AFI 11-235, FARP Operations, to ensure ABFDS and FARP specialists receive the necessary personal equipment

4.2.3. Provides guidance regarding management of DWCF through DoD 4140.25-M and DLA E P-7
4.2.3.1. Assesses and approves waiver requests to requirements outlined in DoD 4140.25-M and DLA Energy Interim Policy Implementation and Procedural Guidance

4.3.3. Provides detailed schematic charts and coordinates with FMT for operating checklists on each fuel system
4.3.4. provides certified base pipeline inventories and strapping charts for each pipeline/fuel tank on installation and coordinates with FMT to meet requirements IAW DLA E P-1

4.4.1.1. Notifies FSC if any contamination is suspected and provides verification of last fuel grade issued to aircraft for defuel
4.4.1.2. Provides flying schedules and promptly notifies FSC of any changes

4.5.1. Conducts required surveys and inspections. Performs safety inspections on the FMF once a year or when requested by FMT.
4.5.1.1. Reviews and approves the selection and use of workplace specific PPE

5.1.1. Comprised of a Fuels Mgmt Flight Commander and a Fuels Manager or Superintendent. At locations that do not earn a FMFC, may reference Fuels Mgmt Flight Leadership. At contracted locations, will be met by contract service provider.
5.1.1.1. Reserved for those in the rank of CMSgt and posses the Primary AF Specialty Code (PAFSC) 2F000
5.1.1.2 Exclusive to those in the rank of SMSgt or MSgt who posses the PAFSC 2F071, attended PLMC or posses 2F091

5.2.2. Immediately notifies AFPA Current Ops (within 2 hours of incident) and submits a follow up message within 24 hours of occurrence with an info copy to the MAJCOM.
5.2.3. Ensures AFPA and DLA regional quality assurance representatives (QARs) are contacted to facilitate disposition instructions.
5.3.3. IAW AFI 36-2201; Completes AF Training Course, Possess a 7-skill level or higher, maintains task quals, proficiencies, and SEI for which individuals are certified, appointed by LRS/CC
5.3.3. Requests and submits Aeronautical Orders to the local HARM office for ABFDS crews once tasked to support UTC JFABF. Further guidance in AFI 11-402

5.3.5. Ensures program comlies with AFI 11-235, Appoints 2F071 as FARP team chief with 035 SEI. Additional duties will not be assigned to team chief. Trains and qualifies personnel to meet mission readiness. Secure storage area for personnel and equipment. Provides monthly FARP program status report to MAJCOM due the first Thursday of the month, certified by FMT

5.3.6. Holds 5-skill level or higher as a qualified fuels specialist. Holds rank of MSgt and below. Be medically qualified according to AFI 44-170 and AFI 48-123 and documented of AF Form 702, classified with code of 9c

5.3.6.5. Completes FARP initial qual course Phase I, II, and III training as well as recurring training with 11-235, posses 6 month experience (time starts when assigned to team), Perform min. of 4 FARP missions and hook ups.

qas.q: Reoccuring and JIT training for ABFDS/FARP operators
qas.a:
5.3.7. Physiological training, initial training conducted via altitude chamber, refresher training every 5 years with ROBD or altitude chamber
Weapons training
Life Support Equip training
Local Aircraft Ground Egress training
Local Night Vision Device training
SERE initial and refresher training
(ECAC FOR ABFDS)
